---
description: Security rules for the DevOpsWes blog repository
applyTo: "**"
---

# Security Rules

## Secrets & Credentials

- **Never** commit secrets, passwords, API keys, tokens, or private keys to the repository.
- This is a public GitHub Pages repository. Anything committed is permanently public.
- Environment-specific config (if ever needed) goes in `.env` files which are gitignored.

## Authentication

- Use SSH key authentication only. Never suggest or configure password-based auth.
- GitHub Actions (if ever added) must use short-lived GITHUB_TOKEN or OIDC, not long-lived PATs stored as secrets.

## Content Security Policy

A CSP meta tag is set in `_includes/head.html`. Do not remove or weaken it.

The `script-src` directive uses two SHA-256 hashes to allow the inline theme-flash prevention script without requiring `'unsafe-inline'`. Two hashes are needed because `git config core.autocrlf=true` on Windows means the file has LF line endings in the git object (served by GitHub Pages) but CRLF when checked out locally — and CSP hashes are computed over the exact bytes the browser receives.

- `sha256-uflw6T8MZO7JuxU6BqdsHHqg/Alzl0rtcykKZ/0cp0w=` — LF line endings (GitHub Pages / production)
- `sha256-3RFK37vAcdEOT89DFY6M0XMP/h02zg0vhGsf1yJ4K1g=` — CRLF line endings (local `jekyll serve` on Windows)

If the inline script in `head.html` is ever modified, both hashes must be recomputed:

```powershell
# Run from the repo root after saving head.html
$raw   = [System.IO.File]::ReadAllBytes("_includes/head.html")
$text  = [System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetString($raw)
$start = $text.IndexOf('<script>') + '<script>'.Length
$end   = $text.IndexOf('</script>')
$crlf  = $text.Substring($start, $end - $start)          # local CRLF version
$lf    = $crlf -replace "`r`n", "`n"                      # git-object LF version

$sha = [System.Security.Cryptography.SHA256]::Create()
"LF   (GitHub Pages) : sha256-$([Convert]::ToBase64String($sha.ComputeHash([System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetBytes($lf))))"
"CRLF (local Windows): sha256-$([Convert]::ToBase64String($sha.ComputeHash([System.Text.Encoding]::UTF8.GetBytes($crlf))))"
```

Both values go into the `script-src` directive, space-separated.

## Google Fonts — SRI Note

SRI (Subresource Integrity) hashes cannot be applied to the Google Fonts CSS link because Google dynamically generates different CSS per browser/user-agent. An SRI hash would cause font load failures across different browsers. Self-hosting the fonts is the only way to add SRI protection — this is a known trade-off accepted for this project.

Google Fonts is loaded from `fonts.googleapis.com` — this is acceptable and intentional.

## Dependencies

- `Gemfile` uses `gem "github-pages"` only. This pins all Jekyll dependencies to the GitHub Pages-approved bundle — intentional and must not be changed.
- Do not add `gem "jekyll"` directly alongside `gem "github-pages"`.
- Do not introduce npm, Node.js build tooling, or other package managers unless explicitly requested. This site builds entirely via Jekyll on GitHub Pages with zero client-side build steps.

## Content Security

- All user-facing content is static HTML generated by Jekyll. There is no server-side code, no database, no form handling.
- Do not add JavaScript that loads remote scripts from untrusted CDNs without a Subresource Integrity (SRI) hash.
- Do not add analytics, tracking pixels, or fingerprinting code. The site has zero tracking by design.

## Personal Information

The author's email (`wes.vanstraten@gmail.com`) and LinkedIn URL are intentionally published on this public professional blog. This is a conscious trade-off accepted by the author. Do not add further PII (internal system hostnames, IP addresses, customer names, internal URLs) to any content.

All case studies and technical posts must be sanitised: no internal URLs, no IP addresses, no customer-identifying details, no confidential system architecture.

## Domain & Hosting

- Custom domain: `www.devopswes.com` (configured in `CNAME` file and GitHub Pages settings).
- Do not modify the `CNAME` file.
- HTTPS is enforced by GitHub Pages.
